skill.name: citation-management
skill.description: Comprehensive citation management for academic research; use when you need to discover papers (Google Scholar/PubMed), extract/verify metadata (DOI/PMID/arXiv/URL), and produce validated, clean BibTeX for manuscripts.
skill.summary: Discover, extract, format, and validate academic citations with BibTeX for research manuscripts.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 998 static findings are false positives from heuristic scanning. The 799 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code formatting markers, not shell execution. The 69 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are BibTeX documentation field names, not actual crypto code. The tool legitimately requires network access for CrossRef/PubMed/arXiv API calls, filesystem for .bib output, and environment variables for API keys. No malicious intent or obfuscation detected.

low_findings.title: Network API calls to hardcoded endpoints
low_findings.description: Scripts make HTTP requests to hardcoded academic API endpoints (CrossRef, PubMed E-utilities, arXiv, doi.org). These are necessary for the tool's citation management function and target legitimate public research APIs.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for API credentials
low_findings.description: Scripts read NCBI_API_KEY and NCBI_EMAIL from environment variables for PubMed API access. This is standard and secure credential management practice for service authentication.

low_findings.title: File system write operations for BibTeX output
low_findings.description: Scripts write formatted BibTeX entries and validation reports to the filesystem. This is expected behavior for a citation management tool that produces .bib output files.

content.user_title: Manage academic citations and generate BibTeX from research papers
content.value_statement: Academic researchers spend hours manually formatting citations for manuscripts. This skill automates paper discovery, metadata extraction, BibTeX generation, and citation validation through PubMed, Google Scholar, CrossRef, and arXiv APIs.
actual_capabilities: Search PubMed and Google Scholar for academic papers with date and filter constraints
Convert DOIs, PMIDs, arXiv IDs and URLs to structured BibTeX entries via CrossRef and PubMed APIs
Validate citation metadata including DOI resolution, required fields, and BibTeX syntax
Format, deduplicate, sort and clean BibTeX libraries for manuscript submission
Extract metadata from mixed identifier lists in batch mode with rate limiting
Fix common BibTeX issues like page ranges, author separators, and DOI formatting
limitations: Google Scholar search requires the optional scholarly Python library which may be rate-limited
Citation data quality depends on the source API (CrossRef, PubMed, arXiv) and metadata availability
BibTeX parsing uses regex and may fail on entries with deeply nested braces or unusual formatting
APA/MLA/etc. output formats are not supported; only BibTeX format is produced

use_cases.title: Build a reference list for a literature review
use_cases.description: Search PubMed or Google Scholar for papers on a topic, extract metadata, generate BibTeX entries, and format them for a manuscript.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate student writing a thesis or literature review

use_cases.title: Clean and validate an existing BibTeX library
use_cases.description: Take a messy .bib file with duplicates and formatting issues, deduplicate entries, fix common errors, and validate all references.
use_cases.target_user: Researcher preparing a manuscript for journal submission

use_cases.title: Convert paper identifiers to citation data
use_cases.description: Take a list of DOIs, PMIDs, or URLs from papers you want to cite and convert them all to properly formatted BibTeX entries in one batch.
use_cases.target_user: Postdoc compiling citations for a grant application

prompt_templates.title: Search and extract citations from PubMed
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the citation-management skill to search PubMed for recent papers on CRISPR gene editing. Search for papers from 2022 to 2024 with a maximum of 50 results. Extract each result into BibTeX format and display the formatted bibliography.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs to quickly gather recent literature on a biomedical topic

prompt_templates.title: Convert a DOI to BibTeX
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the citation-management skill to convert the following DOIs to BibTeX format: 10.1038/s41586-021-03819-2 and 10.1126/science.aam9317. Display each BibTeX entry with proper formatting.
prompt_templates.scenario: An author has a DOI from a paper and needs the BibTeX citation for their manuscript

prompt_templates.title: Validate and fix a BibTeX file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the citation-management skill to validate my bibliography.bib file. Check for missing required fields, duplicate entries, and incorrect DOI formatting. Auto-fix any issues and generate a validation report showing all changes made.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher has a BibTeX file with errors and needs it cleaned before submission

prompt_templates.title: End-to-end citation workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the citation-management skill to perform a complete research workflow. First search PubMed for papers on machine learning in healthcare (2020-2024). Extract BibTeX metadata from the results. Then add these additional DOIs: 10.1038/s41591-023-02200-0. Format the combined bibliography sorted by year with newest first and remove any duplicates.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user needs a reproducible workflow from search to formatted bibliography

output_examples.input: Search PubMed for recent review articles on CRISPR therapy and show the top 5 results
output_examples.output:
Found 142 results for 'CRISPR therapy[Title/Abstract] AND review[Publication Type]' from 2020-2024. Retrieved 5 results:
1. PMID: 34567890 - 'CRISPR-based therapies: current status and future directions' (2023) - Nat Rev Drug Discov
2. PMID: 34567891 - 'Advances in CRISPR gene editing for human diseases' (2022) - Annu Rev Med
3. PMID: 34567892 - 'Delivery strategies for CRISPR-Cas genome editing' (2023) - Nat Rev Clin Oncol
4. PMID: 34567893 - 'Ethical considerations in germline gene editing' (2021) - Nature
5. PMID: 34567894 - 'Base editing: a new frontier in genome engineering' (2022) - Science

output_examples.input: Validate my references.bib file and report any issues
output_examples.output:
BibTeX Validation Report
- File: references.bib | Entries: 24
- Errors: 3 missing required fields, 2 duplicate DOIs, 1 malformed page range
- Warnings: 5 entries missing recommended fields (volume, pages)
- Duplicates removed: 2 entries
- Auto-fixes applied: Fixed 1 page range (123-145 -> 123--145), cleaned 2 author fields
Result: Cleaned file written to references_clean.bib (22 entries)

best_practices: Always include an NCBI API key (NCBI_API_KEY environment variable) for PubMed searches to get faster rate limits of 10 requests per second instead of 3
Validate your BibTeX file before manuscript submission to catch missing required fields, broken DOIs, and duplicate entries early
Use the deduplicate and sort options when combining results from multiple searches to produce a clean, organized bibliography
anti_patterns: Do not skip the validation step after generating BibTeX files; missing fields like journal or pages will cause LaTeX compilation errors
Do not use the Google Scholar search without the scholarly library installed; the script will fail silently or produce empty results
Do not paste raw JSON or API output directly into a manuscript; always convert search results through the extraction step first

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: An NCBI API key is optional but recommended for PubMed searches. Set the NCBI_API_KEY environment variable for faster rate limits. CrossRef and arXiv APIs work without authentication.

faq.question: Can this skill convert citations to APA or MLA format?
faq.answer: No. This skill only produces BibTeX format for LaTeX workflows. It does not support APA, MLA, Chicago, or other non-BibTeX output formats.

faq.question: Does this skill work without internet access?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires internet access to query CrossRef, PubMed, arXiv, and other APIs for metadata. It cannot generate citations from identifiers without network connectivity.

faq.question: What identifier types are supported?
faq.answer: The tool accepts DOIs, PubMed IDs (PMIDs), arXiv IDs, and URLs from arXiv, PubMed, and major publisher websites. It can also parse identifiers from a mixed list in a text file.

faq.question: How does deduplication work?
faq.answer: Deduplication matches entries by DOI first (most reliable), then by citation key. If two entries share the same DOI, only the first one is kept. A warning is printed for each duplicate found.

faq.question: Can I process a batch of citations at once?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide a text file with one identifier per line using the --input flag. The tool will process each one with automatic rate limiting to avoid API throttling.
